summary: American novelist (1933–2023) known for violent, lyrical fiction set in the American South and Southwest. Major works include Blood Meridian (Judge Holden, the Kid, Glanton Gang), All the Pretty Horses (Border Trilogy, John Grady Cole), No Country for Old Men (Anton Chigurh, Llewelyn Moss), The Road (post-apocalyptic father and son), and the Appalachian novels Child of God (Lester Ballard) and Suttree. First four novels set in Appalachia; later work set in the American Southwest and Mexico. The Road won the Pulitzer Prize.

works.name: Blood Meridian
works.description: Western epic (1985) subtitled "The Evening Redness in the West." The Kid, born in Tennessee during a meteor shower, first meets Judge Holden when the Judge crashes a religious revival by accusing the preacher of pedophilia and bestiality. The Kid joins Captain White's filibustering militia into Mexico; after that group is massacred, he is imprisoned in Chihuahua. With the earless Louis Toadvine (who has 'HT' branded on his forehead), the Kid joins the Glanton Gang of scalp hunters. Judge Holden is a giant 7-foot hairless polymath who catalogs wildlife: 'Whatever in creation exists without my knowledge exists without my consent.' He tells the ex-priest Tobin 'War is God' and claims he will never die. He is explicitly associated with the devil. The Kid and Tobin escape; they make improvised gunpowder from urine. The Kid is presumably murdered by the Judge in an outhouse during a meteor shower at the end. The Judge then dances naked with strippers. The world is called 'a hat trick in a medicine show.'

clues.clue: The Kid joins the Glanton Gang under Judge Holden — giant, 7-foot hairless man who claims he will never die; dances naked with strippers at end after murdering the Kid in an outhouse
examples: protagonist joins the Glanton Gang under Judge Holden
a tall, hairless character who furiously plays the fiddle and proclaims that he will never die

clues.clue: Judge Holden says 'Whatever in creation exists without my knowledge exists without my consent' and 'War is God' (to ex-priest Tobin)
examples: A man in this novel explains that he exhaustively catalogs flora and fauna because 'Whatever in creation exists without my knowledge exists without my consent'

clues.clue: The Kid born during a meteor shower in Tennessee; Kid first meets Judge when Judge crashes a religious revival accusing the preacher of bestiality

works.name: All the Pretty Horses
works.description: First novel of the Border Trilogy. John Grady Cole leaves his grandfather's ranch in San Angelo, Texas (after the grandfather's death) and runs away to Mexico with friend Lacey Rawlins. They meet teenager Jimmy Blevins, who kills a man while retrieving his beloved Colt pistol; John Grady later tries to find the owner of Blevins's horse. John Grady has an affair with Alejandra, daughter of the ranch owner; her father betrays them to the authorities. John Grady stabs a cuchillero to death in a prison knife fight after discussing countries with Perez; he uses a heated pistol barrel to cauterize a gunshot wound during escape. Blevins is hanged. John Grady cauterizes his wound with a hot pistol barrel.

clues.clue: John Grady Cole runs away to Mexico with friend Rawlins from his dead grandfather's ranch; has affair with Alejandra, whose father betrays them to authorities
examples: the protagonist has an affair with Alejandra after running away to Mexico from his dead grandfather's ranch
John Grady Cole in All the Pretty Horses

clues.clue: Jimmy Blevins is a 13-year-old who claims to be deaf in one ear after being struck by lightning; shoots hole through tossed wallet; kills a man during retrieval of his Colt pistol; is hanged
examples: a 13-year-old character who claims to be deaf in one ear after being struck by lightning twice

clues.clue: John Grady stabs a cuchillero to death in a prison knife fight after discussing countries with Perez; cauterizes gunshot wound with heated pistol barrel during escape
examples: stabs a cuchillero to death during a knife and cafeteria tray fight in prison

works.name: No Country for Old Men
works.description: Novel (2005), originally conceived as a screenplay. Llewelyn Moss finds $2.4 million in heroin money near the Mexican border (after meeting a dying Mexican man). The killer Anton Chigurh pursues him; Chigurh carries an air-powered captive bolt stunner ('cattle gun') and flips coins to decide victims' fates. He kills Carla Jean saying 'You didn't do anything. It was bad luck.' Bounty hunter Carson Wells offers Moss protection while he recovers in a Mexican hospital. Sheriff Ed Bell narrates; the novel ends with Bell recounting a dream where his dead father carried a moon-colored horn lit with fire. Title from the Yeats poem 'Sailing to Byzantium.' Javier Bardem played Chigurh in the Coen brothers' acclaimed film adaptation. Harold Bloom compared Chigurh unfavorably to Judge Holden.

clues.clue: Anton Chigurh pursues Llewelyn Moss who found $2.4M near Mexican border; Chigurh uses captive bolt stunner, flips coins; kills Carla Jean
examples: the killer Anton Chigurh is hunted by Sheriff Ed Bell
Anton Chigurh... flips a coin to decide his victims' fates

clues.clue: Title from Yeats's 'Sailing to Byzantium'; Coen brothers film adaptation with Javier Bardem as Chigurh
examples: takes its title from the Yeats poem 'Sailing to Byzantium'
Javier Bardem played the hitman Anton Chigurh in the acclaimed Coen brothers film adaptation

clues.clue: Sheriff Bell narrates; novel ends with Bell's dream of his dead father carrying a moon-colored horn lit with fire
examples: This novel ends with one protagonist recounting a dream in which his dead father carried a moon-colored horn lit with fire

clues.clue: Originally conceived as a screenplay; bounty hunter Carson Wells offers Moss protection in Mexican hospital
examples: Cormac McCarthy originally conceived of the idea for this novel about a botched drug deal as a screenplay

works.name: The Road
works.description: Post-apocalyptic novel (2006), winner of the Pulitzer Prize. An unnamed father and son travel south through a devastated American landscape, surviving on scavenged food. The father has only two bullets; uses one to kill a cannibal. He tells his son they are 'carrying the fire' and are 'the good guys.' They discover a wall of human heads and human infants roasted on spits. They find a bunker full of food (peaches, pears, biscuits) and thank 'the people.' An old man who claims his name is Ely appears. The mother had walked into the wilderness and killed herself with a piece of obsidian after an argument about the father's two bullets. The father is shot by an arrow and uses a flare gun; he is also shot by a diesel truck driver and later whittles wooden bullets. The father dies telling his son to 'carry the fire'; the son is taken in by a family. The novel ends with an intricate description of brook trout with 'vermiculate patterns that were maps of the world in its becoming' on their backs. McCarthy was inspired by seeing 'fires on the hill' while visiting El Paso with his son. Set about ten years after an unnamed apocalyptic event.

clues.clue: Unnamed father and son travel post-apocalyptic America; 'carrying the fire'; father has two bullets; finds bunker of food; ends with brook trout whose backs are 'maps of the world in its becoming'
examples: a father and son journey down the titular structure, written by Cormac McCarthy
father reassures his son that they are the 'good guys' who are 'carrying the fire'

clues.clue: Protagonists find human infant cooked on a spit; wall of human heads; cannibals with slaves and pregnant women
examples: This novel's protagonists find a human infant being cooked on a spit

clues.clue: Mother walked into wilderness to kill herself with a piece of obsidian; an argument over the two bullets precipitates this
examples: The protagonist's wife had left to kill herself with obsidian, and he later shoots a man from a diesel truck, leaving just one bullet in his gun

clues.clue: Old man claims name is Ely; protagonists give him food; father notes 'no more crows' after thinking about crows
examples: a character in this novel gives food to an old man who claims his name is Ely

clues.clue: Won the Pulitzer Prize; inspired by McCarthy seeing 'fires on the hill' while visiting El Paso, Texas with his son
examples: While visiting this city with his son, McCarthy described seeing 'fires on the hill,' which inspired the plot of The Road

works.name: Suttree
works.description: Semi-autobiographical novel. Cornelius Suttree abandons his luxurious life to live as a catfish-seller in a houseboat in McAnally Flats on the Tennessee River in Knoxville. Gene Harrogate ('the Moonlight Melonmounter') is sent to a work camp after violating a farmer's watermelons; he befriends Suttree. Suttree Landing park on the Tennessee River is dedicated to McCarthy.

clues.clue: Cornelius Suttree abandons his luxurious life to live as a catfish-seller on a houseboat in Knoxville's McAnally Flats along the Tennessee River
examples: Cornelius, the title character, leaves his luxurious life to live in solitude as a catfish-seller in Knoxville along the Tennessee River

clues.clue: Gene Harrogate (the 'Moonlight Melonmounter') sent to work camp for violating watermelons; befriends Suttree
examples: Gene Harrogate was sent to a work camp after he 'violated' a farmer's watermelons

works.name: Child of God
works.description: Third novel. Lester Ballard, described as 'a child of God much like yourself perhaps,' is a dim-witted necrophile and serial killer in Sevier County, Tennessee. After losing his family land, he moves into caves and murders women at Frog Mountain turnaround, keeping their bodies in the cave. Opens with a house auction where the evicted tenant assaults the auctioneer. A dump keeper with nine daughters appears. The novel ends with Ballard's body being dissected by medical students; seven bodies are removed from the cave.

clues.clue: Lester Ballard — serial killer 'much like yourself perhaps'; lives in a mountain cave in Tennessee keeping bodies of murdered women; necrophile; novel ends with his body dissected and seven bodies removed from cave
examples: created a serial killer 'much like yourself perhaps' named Lester Ballard
moves into a mountainous cave, where he begins keeping the bodies of women he murders

works.name: The Border Trilogy
works.description: All the Pretty Horses, The Crossing, and Cities of the Plain. The Crossing begins with protagonist Billy Parham taking a pregnant wolf from New Mexico back to Mexico; the protagonist cries after failing to call back a badly injured dog. Cities of the Plain ends with John Grady Cole falling in love with an epileptic prostitute at The White Lake; her owner kills him in a knife fight.

clues.clue: The Crossing — protagonist Billy Parham takes a pregnant wolf back to Mexico; cries after failing to call back an injured dog
examples: Billy Parham takes a pregnant one of these animals from New Mexico to Mexico in the first section of Cormac McCarthy's novel The Crossing

clues.clue: Cities of the Plain — John Grady falls in love with epileptic prostitute at The White Lake; owner kills him in knife fight
examples: falls in love with an epileptic prostitute working at The White Lake, whose owner kills him in a knife fight

works.name: Outer Dark
works.description: Second novel. Siblings Culla and Rinthy. Three unnamed characters burn a child in the same site where they hang a character known as the Tinker.

clues.clue: Three unnamed characters burn a child in same site where they hang the Tinker; sibling protagonists Culla and Rinthy
examples: three unnamed characters burn a child in the same site where they hang a character known as the Tinker

works.name: The Passenger / Stella Maris
works.description: 2022 companion novels. The Passenger follows two mathematical prodigies whose father worked on the atomic bomb; Stella Maris was written as a 'coda' to The Passenger. A man in The Passenger reminisces on his schizophrenic sister's hallucinations of the Thalidomide Kid. A woman in Stella Maris cries while reading a letter that reveals the location of a lost Amati violin. McCarthy dedicated an early copy of a novel to his friend John Sheddan, whose death in an empty theater is fictionalized in The Passenger.

clues.clue: The Passenger (2022) and Stella Maris — two mathematical prodigies; sister hallucinates the Thalidomide Kid; Stella Maris is a 'coda' to The Passenger
examples: Stella Maris was written as a 'coda' to The Passenger
two mathematical prodigies whose father worked on the atomic bomb

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Cormac McCarthy (1933–2023) was an American novelist. His first novel was The Orchard Keeper; his first four novels were set in Appalachia, later work in the American Southwest and Mexico. He moved to El Paso, Texas in 1976. He died in June 2023. The Coen brothers adapted No Country for Old Men and reportedly modeled Barton Fink on him. The Tennessee River park Suttree Landing is dedicated to him in Knoxville.

clues.clue: First four novels set in Appalachia (Outer Dark, Child of God, Suttree, The Orchard Keeper); later work in American Southwest and Mexico
examples: set their first four novels in Appalachia

clues.clue: Coen brothers adapted No Country for Old Men; reportedly modeled Barton Fink on McCarthy
examples: The Coen brothers modeled Barton Fink on this playwright

clues.clue: Died June 2023; moved to El Paso, Texas in 1976; inspired by seeing 'fires on the hill' there for The Road
examples: While visiting this city with his son, McCarthy described seeing 'fires on the hill,' which inspired the plot of The Road

comprehensive_summary:
Cormac McCarthy (1933–2023) was an American novelist known for violent, lyrical fiction. His first four novels were set in Appalachia; later work shifted to the American Southwest and Mexico. He moved to El Paso, Texas in 1976 and died in June 2023.

Blood Meridian (1985), subtitled 'The Evening Redness in the West,' follows the Kid, who is born in Tennessee during a meteor shower. The Kid first encounters Judge Holden when the Judge crashes a religious revival by accusing the preacher of pedophilia and bestiality. The Kid joins Captain White's filibustering militia into Mexico before joining the Glanton Gang of scalp hunters. Judge Holden is a giant, 7-foot, hairless polymath who extensively catalogs wildlife ('Whatever in creation exists without my knowledge exists without my consent') and tells the ex-priest Tobin 'War is God.' He is explicitly associated with the devil and claims he will never die. The Kid and Tobin escape by making improvised gunpowder from urine. The Kid is presumably murdered by the Judge in an outhouse during a meteor shower; the Judge then dances naked with strippers.

All the Pretty Horses, the first of the Border Trilogy, follows John Grady Cole from San Angelo, Texas, who runs away to Mexico with friend Lacey Rawlins after his grandfather's death. They meet Jimmy Blevins, a 13-year-old who claims lightning deafened him in one ear and who kills a man while retrieving his Colt pistol. John Grady has an affair with Alejandra, whose ranch-owner father betrays them to the authorities. John Grady stabs a cuchillero to death in a prison knife fight and cauterizes his escape wound with a heated pistol barrel. The Crossing begins with Billy Parham taking a pregnant wolf back to Mexico and ends with him crying after failing to call back an injured dog. Cities of the Plain ends with John Grady falling in love with an epileptic prostitute at The White Lake; her owner kills him in a knife fight.

No Country for Old Men (2005), originally conceived as a screenplay, follows Llewelyn Moss who finds $2.4 million in drug money near the Mexican border. The killer Anton Chigurh, who carries an air-powered captive bolt stunner and flips coins to decide victims' fates, pursues him. Bounty hunter Carson Wells offers Moss protection in a Mexican hospital. Chigurh kills Carla Jean saying 'You didn't do anything. It was bad luck.' Sheriff Ed Bell narrates; the novel ends with his dream of his dead father carrying a moon-colored horn lit with fire. Title from Yeats's 'Sailing to Byzantium.' Javier Bardem played Chigurh in the acclaimed Coen brothers adaptation.

The Road (2006), winner of the Pulitzer Prize, follows an unnamed father and son traveling south through post-apocalyptic America. The father tells his son they are 'carrying the fire' and are 'the good guys.' They have only two bullets. They encounter a wall of human heads, infants roasted on spits, and a caravan of cannibals keeping slaves. They find a bunker full of food and thank 'the people.' An old man claims his name is Ely. The mother earlier walked into the wilderness and killed herself with a piece of obsidian. The father dies and the son is taken in by a family. The novel ends with an intricate description of brook trout with 'vermiculate patterns that were maps of the world in its becoming.' McCarthy was inspired by seeing 'fires on the hill' while visiting El Paso with his son.

Suttree is a semi-autobiographical novel in which Cornelius Suttree abandons his luxurious family life to live as a catfish-seller on a houseboat in McAnally Flats on Knoxville's Tennessee River. His friend Gene Harrogate ('the Moonlight Melonmounter') was sent to a work camp for violating watermelons. Suttree Landing park in Knoxville is dedicated to McCarthy.

Child of God (third novel) follows Lester Ballard, a dim-witted serial killer and necrophile in Sevier County, Tennessee, who moves into caves and murders women, keeping their bodies; the novel ends with his body dissected by medical students. Outer Dark features siblings Culla and Rinthy; three unnamed characters burn a child and hang the Tinker. The Passenger and Stella Maris (2022) follow mathematical prodigies whose father worked on the atomic bomb; the sister hallucinates the Thalidomide Kid.

The Coen brothers adapted No Country for Old Men and reportedly modeled Barton Fink on McCarthy.
